verse_text: Will not the land be disturbed by these things, and will not everyone dwelling in it mourn? Even as a flooding river comes to an end, so too the flood waters of the river of Egypt return to their banks.

links.context: The Greek word for mourned (pentheo) is also used in the Septuagint to refer to (1) grieving for the dead, and (2) sorrow over unrepentant sinners (see [[NEH.8:9]]; Is 24:4; Dan 10:2; [[AMO.8:8]]). Taken away from among you refers to excommunication.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
